layman_headline: Fear grips crypto markets — no clear setup worth risking capital on right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is sitting in 'Fear' territory at 26/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.2%, meaning altcoins are getting beaten up disproportionately. Most swing candidates on the watchlist are in downtrends with bearish chart structures. While TRUMP has an oversold RSI at 29 which historically signals a bounce, the chart is firmly bearish with price below all major moving averages. The best move right now is to stay patient and wait for a cleaner setup.
layman_action: Keep your cash ready but don't deploy it yet. The market is fearful and most altcoins are trending down. Wait for either a genuine capitulation flush that creates a clear bounce setup, or for fear to start turning toward neutral with improving chart structures.
expert_reasoning: Surveying all 10 candidates: USDG is a stablecoin (no trade), HTX has a bullish chart (+1.1 bias) but RSI at 58 is already extended after a +5% week with microscopic price making it untradeable. MON is up +13% with no chart data. ETHFI shows bullish Marubozu but R/R is only 0.11x with resistance at $0.40 right overhead. GRASS dropped -25% in a week but has a bearish engulfing pattern and -0.9 chart bias — a falling knife. TRUMP's RSI at 29 is the strongest single signal, but price is below all MAs in a clear downtrend with support only 0.6% away at $1.60 meaning a break could cascade lower. With BTC dominance at 56.2% and Fear & Greed at 26, the environment actively punishes altcoin longs. I'm waiting for either TRUMP to test $1.50 with a reversal candle, or for broader market fear to shift before deploying capital.
risk_factors: High BTC dominance (56.2%) actively suppresses altcoin performance — alts could continue bleeding
Fear & Greed at 26 and improving slowly, but could easily reverse lower if macro events escalate (Iran strikes mentioned in news)
All swing candidates either have bearish chart structures, are already extended, or lack sufficient data for high-conviction entries
watch_list: TRUMP RSI — if it drops below 25 with a hammer/morning star candle near $1.50, that's a high-conviction oversold bounce entry
BTC price action around the Fidelity power law support line — a confirmed bounce there would improve the entire alt landscape
Fear & Greed index — a move above 30 toward neutral would signal improving conditions for new entries
market_context: Crypto markets are in a fear-driven environment with BTC dominance at 56.2% crushing altcoin performance. Bitcoin is relatively stable (+2% weekly) but alts are broadly declining 5-25% on the week, creating a hostile environment for swing longs on smaller tokens.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and persistent fear create a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades — capital preservation takes priority over forced entries.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') with a slowly improving trend from 24 weekly avg — not yet at levels that reliably mark bottoms for aggressive buying.
TRUMP's RSI at 29 is the only genuinely oversold reading across all candidates, but lacks bullish confirmation candles to trigger entry.
Bitcoin nearing Fidelity's power law support line tracked since 2015 — a potential macro floor that could stabilize BTC and by extension alts.
Jamie Coutts of Real Vision suggests Bitcoin is in late stages of a bear market, implying a potential macro reversal is approaching but not confirmed.
Most candidates show bearish chart bias scores (GRASS -0.9, PENGU -0.7, TRUMP -0.7, XPL -0.4) — the market structure broadly favors sellers across altcoins.
